task: Launch-quality advanced task: Coherent Fiber Link Polarization Drift Alarm Tuning. Reality context: a coherent optical link triggers excessive alarms after a route repair. Embedded packet: metric map: metric1=sop_rotation_deg_s, metric2=ber_e12, metric3=osnr_dB, metric4=alarm_count_day; records: R1 sop_rotation_deg_s=10.4 ber_e12=78.2 osnr_dB=6.1 alarm_count_day=476; R2 sop_rotation_deg_s=53.9 ber_e12=26.4 osnr_dB=25 alarm_count_day=294; R3 sop_rotation_deg_s=27.2 ber_e12=27.51 osnr_dB=6.7 alarm_count_day=452; R4 sop_rotation_deg_s=48.5 ber_e12=70.28 osnr_dB=26.3 alarm_count_day=681; limit_sop_rotation_deg_s=4.7; limit_ber_e12=34.11; limit_osnr_dB=28.8; decision_budget_usd=160000; sensitivity_shift_pct=5; hard_threshold_record_count=1; removed_context="benchmark rewarded verifiable intermediate outputs, not only final answers". Rules and constraints: Separate safety/compliance blockers from economic optimizations; blockers override ROI, while nonblockers are ranked by expected value per unit cost. Options or recommendations cannot use external measurements, specialized licensed software, or hidden assumptions; all calculations must use only the embedded packet, and any contradictory or under-supported record must be marked needs_review. Required work: retune alarms while preserving fault detection; rank records by technical and economic impact, run the stated sensitivity check, and recommend hold, release, retest, or mitigation. Deliver polarization_alarm_tuning.xlsx + noc_policy.md with formulas or executable logic, record-level citations, validation checks, sensitivity notes, and a final recommendation table suitable for expert review.
